data.title: NEXTNRG 10-K: $81.8M Revenue, $(0.72) EPS on $88.2M Net Loss
data.description:
NEXTNRG reported fiscal-year net revenue of $81.835 million and a basic and diluted loss per share of $(0.72), reflecting a substantial year-over-year revenue increase driven by higher volumes and pricing but a steep overall net loss of $(88.176) million due to elevated operating expenses and investments in scale. Gross margin improved to $6.907 million from $1.787 million a year earlier, while operating loss widened, driven by higher depreciation and other scale-up costs. The company is emphasizing membership recurring revenue and expanded delivery capacity as it scales operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Sales – net: $81.835 million, up $54.065 million (194.69%) versus prior year per MD&A.
  • Gross margin: $6.907 million, improved from $1.787 million in the prior year.
  • Loss from operations: $(70.193) million (operating loss increased versus prior year).
  • Net loss: $(88.176) million, $(66.779) million worse year-over-year (312.10% change).
  • Basic and diluted loss per share: $(0.72); weighted average shares outstanding: 122.110 million.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers: Revenues surged approximately 195% year-over-year driven by higher gallons delivered and increased average price per gallon.
  • Channel & customer mix: Expansion into new regions and strengthened commercial fleet partnerships boosted recurring contracted deliveries.
  • Brand & technology: App enhancements and targeted marketing improved customer acquisition, scheduling, and order conversion.
  • Operational scale-up: Significant investment in delivery vehicles and capital assets expanded capacity and contributed to higher depreciation expense.
  • Membership model: Monthly memberships provide recurring revenue recognized over one-month cycles, supporting predictable short-term revenue streams.

Original SEC Filing: NEXTNRG, INC. [ NXXT ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Jocom Holdings 10-K: Revenue $0, Net loss $(839,842), EPS not disclosed
data.description:
Jocom Holdings reported zero revenue for the fiscal year, a steep decline from $24,000 in the prior year, and posted a net loss of $839,842 (basic and diluted EPS not numerically disclosed; weighted average shares 64,632,881). The operating loss of $937,220 was driven by increased general and administrative expenses, including a $648,000 impairment. The company is shifting strategy toward growth via business combinations and potential biotech opportunities.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 in 2025 vs $24,000 in 2024 — decline attributed to change of directors and direction.
  • Gross Profit: $0 in 2025 vs $24,000 in 2024.
  • Operating Income: Operating loss of $(937,220) in 2025; loss driven by G&A expenses and includes a $648,000 impairment.
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(839,842) in 2025 vs net profit of $68,519 in 2024.
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic and diluted per-share amounts not numerically disclosed; weighted average shares outstanding 64,632,881 for 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue fell to zero as the company shifted strategy following director changes.
  • Strategic Direction: Transitioning away from a data-analytics/grocery SaaS focus toward growth through business combinations and exploring potential acquisition in the biotech sector.
  • Operational Milestones: Entered a nondisclosure agreement for a China biotech target in June 2025 and began negotiations for an AI-driven insect-repellent collaboration.
  • Management & Restructuring: New CEO appointed August 22, 2025; restructuring and increased general and administrative activity prompted an impairment of receivables that affected operations.

Original SEC Filing: JOCOM HOLDINGS CORP. [ JOCM ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: Eurozone Inflation for March Revised Higher on Sharper Energy Prices — Update
data.description:
By Ed Frankl

Inflation in the eurozone was a bit higher in March than previously thought, as the energy shock from the Iran war proved more pronounced than expected.

Consumer prices were up 2.6% on year last month, the highest level since July 2024 and up from 1.9% in February. It was also an upward revision from the provisional estimate of 2.5% published on March 31.

Energy inflation for March was revised up to 5.1% from the 4.9% previously reported. In February, energy was 3.1% cheaper than a year prior. Meanwhile, core inflation, which strips out more volatile energy and food costs, was unchanged from the flash reading at 2.3%.

The second estimate of inflation takes a more comprehensive overview of the data, including a higher coverage of subsectors used to calculate the overall figure.

"Inflation is headed for 3%, and it will stay close to this level for a while," Claus Vistesen, chief eurozone economist at Pantheon Macroeconomics, said in a note to clients.

While the impact of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz on inflation is already evident, much about future effects will depend on the duration and extent of the war. While central banks sometimes see pronounced increases in energy costs as temporary, they can grow more concerned if price pressures spread to more persistent forms of second-round inflation via, for instance,spiraling wage costs.

"A significant inflation shock is still on the way, even if we assume relatively modest second-round effects in food and core inflation," Vistesen said.

European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said this week that the impact of the war was landing between the bank's baseline and adverse scenarios, the latter of which predicts a sharper rise in inflation and slower economic growth.

"We have to be completely agile and ready to move in the direction that is required," she said on the sidelines of an International Monetary Fund meeting in Washington, D.C.

The bank, which said in March that it expected inflation to average 2.6% this year, next meets on April 30. Investors have ramped up bets on interest-rate rises since the first strikes on Iran in late February. They have priced in at least two quarter-point hikes this year, according to LSEG data.

The IMF on Tuesday downgraded its forecast for eurozone growth this year to 1.1% from the 1.3% growth it forecast in January.

Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com

data.title: Domo 10-K: $318.9M Revenue, $(1.45) EPS
data.description:
Domo reported fiscal 2026 revenue of $318.9 million and a net loss per share of $(1.45), with the company narrowing its net loss to $(59.3) million from $(81.9) million a year earlier. Revenue was essentially flat year-over-year driven by steady subscription revenue, while improved operating efficiency reduced the loss from operations. Domo continues to emphasize consumption-based pricing and product investment to drive longer-term ARR stability.

Financial Highlights

  • Total revenue: $318.9 million (≈1% YoY increase from $317.0 million)
  • Gross profit: $239.1 million; gross margin 75%
  • Loss from operations: $(39.1) million (improved from $(59.3) million prior year)
  • Net loss: $(59.3) million (improved from $(81.9) million prior year)
  • Net loss per share: $(1.45); weighted-average shares 40.984 million
  • Accumulated deficit: $1,546.9 million as of January 31, 2026

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix: Subscription revenue comprised approximately 91% of total revenue and remained roughly flat at ~$289 million.
  • Pricing model shift: Significant transition to consumption-based pricing with 84% of ARR on a consumption model to increase predictability.
  • Customer base & retention: Over 2,400 customers; ARR net retention improved to 96% by Q4 2026, with enterprise revenue declining while corporate revenue increased.
  • Product and R&D: More than $1.006 billion invested in the platform since inception; ongoing investments in AI/ML and Appstore capabilities with R&D headcount at 263.
  • Operational efficiency: Management reduced sales & marketing and R&D spend as a percentage of revenue and focused on sales productivity and expanding the partner ecosystem.

Original SEC Filing: DOMO, INC. [ DOMO ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: Data443 Risk Mitigation 10-K: $4.42M Revenue, $(2.57)M Net Loss
data.description:
Data443 Risk Mitigation reported $4.421 million in revenue for the fiscal year, a 9% decline from the prior year driven by one-time catch-up payments in the prior period, and posted a net loss of $(2.568) million that benefited from settlement gains. Gross profit was $2.286 million with a 52% margin, and operating loss totaled $(2.131) million. The results reflect ongoing shifts in go-to-market strategy and integration of acquired products as the company focuses on recurring revenue and cost efficiencies.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $4.421 million, decrease of $0.451 million (−9%) versus 2024; decline driven by one-time catch-up payments in prior year.
  • Gross Profit: $2.286 million; gross margin 52% (versus 58% in 2024).
  • Operating Income: Operating loss of $(2.131) million (operating loss of $2,130,967 as stated).
  • Net Income: Net loss of $(2.568) million, improvement of $3.519 million (−58%) versus 2024, reflecting settlement gains.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Reported revenue declined ~9% year-over-year due to prior-year catch-up payments, while annual recurring revenue (ARR) shows organic consumption growth.
  • Go-to-Market: Continued shift to channel-led and inbound sales with emphasis on renewals, customer success, and re-engaging customers from acquired products.
  • Product & Marketplace Momentum: Expanded product suite to include threat intelligence, privacy, ransomware recovery, TacitRed, and Vaikora, and onboarded offerings to Azure and AWS marketplaces to increase visibility.
  • Operational Efficiency: Integrated multiple acquisitions, consolidated data center costs, reduced consulting spend, and centralized cloud-native deployments to improve operational efficiency.

Original SEC Filing: Data443 Risk Mitigation, Inc. [ ATDS ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

data.title: China Foods Holdings 10-K: Revenue $0.33M, EPS $(0.02)
data.description:
China Foods Holdings reported fiscal-year revenue of $327,871, a 40.5% increase from the prior year, while basic and diluted net loss per share remained $(0.02). The company posted a net loss of $(0.399) million, an improvement from the prior-year loss of $(0.456) million, with operating losses narrowing alongside reduced operating expenses.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $327,871 (increase of 40.5% vs prior year; prior year $233,339)
  • Gross Profit: $29,962 (decreased from $53,873; decline attributed to lower wine sales)
  • Operating Income: Loss from operations $(0.399) million; operating expenses decreased (G&A down from $502,654 to $423,644)
  • Net Income: Net loss $(0.399) million, improved from prior-year net loss of $(0.456) million
  • Net Income Per Share: Basic and diluted $(0.02), unchanged from prior year

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Higher demand in wine and healthcare product sales supported the 40.5% revenue increase year-over-year.
  • Channel Expansion: Expanded online presence through Tmall, WeChat, TikTok and Xiaohongshu while continuing distributor-led offline sales.
  • Product and Brand Momentum: Broadened product mix to include supplements, meal replacements, skincare and high-end wine; launched nutrition consulting via social media channels.
  • Operational Efficiency: Focused C2M (consumer-to-manufacturer) supply-chain collaboration to reduce logistics and inventory costs and reduced G&A expenses, notably rent.

Original SEC Filing: China Foods Holdings Ltd. [ CFOO ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Catalyst Crew Technologies 2025 10-K: $0 Revenue, $(0.01) EPS
data.description:
Catalyst Crew Technologies reported no revenue for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024 and posted a net loss of $207,485 in 2025, or $(0.01) per share, driven by operating expenses that produced an operating loss of $171,852. The company reported an improved net loss versus $3,261,038 in 2024 but continues to have no commercialization revenue. Management cites substantial doubt about the company's ability to continue as a going concern and plans to prioritize fundraising and licensing to resume operations.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 for the years ended December 31, 2025 and 2024.
  • Operating Income: $(171,852) loss from operations for the year ended December 31, 2025.
  • Net Income: $(207,485) net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025 (improved from $(3,261,038) in 2024).
  • Net Income Per Share: $(0.01) net loss per common share — basic and diluted for the year ended December 31, 2025.

Business Highlights

  • Commercialization Status: No revenue recognized in 2025 or 2024, indicating no near-term sales traction or commercialization to date.
  • Operational Cost Drivers: Operating expenses increased notably in 2024 due in part to higher stock-based compensation, reflecting investment in personnel and retention efforts.
  • Strategic Direction: Management intends to pursue licensing agreements and seek private equity or debt financing to support operations and future commercialization efforts.
  • Going Concern and Priorities: The company disclosed substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern and will prioritize fundraising and product licensing to attempt to resume and sustain operations.

Original SEC Filing: Catalyst Crew Technologies Corp. [ CCTC ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: Capstone Holding 2025 10-K: Revenue $46.9M, Net Loss $(21.23)M
data.description:
Capstone Holding reported fiscal 2025 net sales of $46.9 million, up $2.0 million (4.5%) year-over-year, while recording a net loss of $21.23 million driven by impairment charges, valuation allowance and higher interest and SG&A. Gross profit improved to $10.791 million with a 23.0% margin, but operating results were hurt by increased selling, general and administrative expenses and goodwill impairment. Management highlighted acquisitions completed during the year as key contributors to revenue growth and expanded operational reach.

Financial Highlights

  • Net Sales: $46.9 million, up $2.0 million (+4.5%) versus prior year, driven by acquisitions.
  • Gross Profit: $10.791 million; margin improved to 23.0%.
  • Loss from Operations: $(10.986) million, a decline of $(10.348) million attributable to higher SG&A and goodwill impairment.
  • Net Loss: $(21.230) million, down $(18.667) million due to impairment, valuation allowance and higher interest and SG&A.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not stated explicitly in Part II, Item 7/Item 8 and therefore omitted.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Growth Drivers: Two acquisitions completed in 2025 materially contributed to top-line growth and inventory expansion.
  • Channel and Geographic Expansion: Broadened U.S. distribution footprint and expanded Canadian operations, increasing dealer reach and regional coverage.
  • Service Offering Expansion: Added turnkey installation capability through the acquisition of Carolina Stone (closed in August).
  • Product & Brand Mix: Expanded portfolio across natural and manufactured stone, thin brick and hardscape products and promoted proprietary brands to support improved gross margins.
  • Operational Scale: Completed acquisitions of Carolina Stone and Fraser Canyon/CSI (closed in December), increasing inventory and dealer relationships to support seasonal demand peaks in Q2–Q3.
  • Strategy & Outlook: Management is pursuing organic growth and additional acquisitions to build scale and leverage seasonal demand, while integrating recent acquisitions to drive future operational performance.

Original SEC Filing: Capstone Holding Corp. [ CAPS ] - 10-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: ZAWYA: LSE-listed Dar Global secures $250mln term loan
data.description:
Staff Writer

Dar Global, the LSE-listed real estate developer, has secured a $250 million syndicated term loan facility from Emirates NBD Capital to fund its expansion plans across “key global markets”.

Dar Global, which is majority owned by Saudi Arabia’s Dar Al Arkan Real Estate Development, said the funding will also accelerate existing projects and pursue new property developments.

Emirates NBD Capital acted as the joint mandated lead arranger, bookrunner and sole documentation agent on the deal.

In February, Dar Global announced it had held meetings in China to expand cross-border investment partnerships as Saudi Arabia’s real estate market opens to foreign non-resident investors.

(Writing by Bindu Rai, editing by Seban Scaria)

bindu.rai@lseg.com

data.title: What AI picked up about central bankers' tone as geopolitics shake macro outlook
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 up 0.2%
  • Optimism about Mid east talks picks back up
  • Wall Street futures higher

WHAT AI PICKED UP ABOUT CENTRAL BANK TONE AS GEOPOLITICS SHAKE MACRO OUTLOOK

The Middle East conflict has upended the global macro outlook - and UBS finds central banks are striking uneven tones.

A team of economists used an AI large-language model tracker to measure policy tone for the Federal Reserve, European Central Bank, and Bank of Japan.

At the Fed, UBS sees communication moving away from a prolonged dovish phase.

After initially striking a dovish tone since late summer 2025, Fed communication has shifted more hawkish since the recent geopolitical shock, led by a renewed focus on inflation. But critically this shift in language has not been matched by weaker language on growth or the labour market, leaving the overall signal still dovish, though slowly trending higher.

Across speakers, Chair Jerome Powell stands out as the most hawkish voice, followed by New York Fed President John Williams. Fed Governor Michael Barr remains firmly on the dovish side according to UBS's AI analysis.

At the ECB, UBS says a sharp spike in hawkishness following the start of the Middle East conflict has been sustained, but that has now started to ease.

"Growth and labour tone, which were on an upward trend entering the shock, have now flattened, offsetting some of the persistent hawkish inflation impulse," the analysts say.

Among policymakers, Isabel Schnabel, Gabriel Makhlouf and Joachim Nagel cluster at the hawkish end, while Olli Rehn is more dovish, according to UBS, while President Christine Lagarde sits close to the centre of the distribution.

At the BOJ, UBS says the earlier hawkish drive has paused. Inflation remains the main driver of tone, but there are signs of greater caution around growth and policy, with inflation rhetoric itself beginning to plateau.

Recent messaging has also been sparse, leaving UBS to conclude the BOJ is entering a consolidation phase, rather than extending its previous hawkish shift.

All three central banks meeting in the final week of April to discuss monetary policy. Money markets show traders are attaching a 20% chance of an ECB rate hike and a roughly 18% chance the BOJ raises rates, while the Fed is widely expected to make no change to monetary policy at all, according to LSEG data.

data.title: Pound Rises Against Majors
data.description:
BRUSSELS (dpa-AFX) - The British pound strengthened against other major currencies in the European session on Thursday.

The pound rose to 1.3582 against the U.S. dollar, from an early low of 1.3561. Earlier, the pound had risen to nearly a 2-month high of 1.3595 against the greenback.

Against the yen, the pound advanced to 215.73 from an early low of 215.13.

Against the Swiss franc and the euro, the pound edged up to 1.0610 and 0.8691 from early lows of 1.0597 and 0.8702, respectively.

If the pound extends its uptrend, it is likely to find resistance around 1.37 against the greenback, 216.00 against the yen, 1.07 against the franc and 0.85 against the euro.

Copyright(c) 2026 RTTNews.com. All Rights Reserved

Copyright RTT News/dpa-AFX

data.title: Rupee edges higher as optimism over Iran peace deal firms
data.description:
By Jaspreet Kalra

The Indian rupee closed slightly stronger on Thursday on growing optimism over a potential deal to end the Iran war, which kept oil prices below $100 a barrel and lifted global equities to record highs.

The rupee closed at 93.1950 against the U.S. dollar, up 0.2% from its previous close.

Traders said the price action was largely driven by client flows, aided by positive cues on the Iran war and tighter limits on banks' net open positions, which curtailed appetite for wagers against the currency.

Optimism grew on Thursday that the war in the Middle East may be nearing an end, with a key Pakistani mediator in Tehran and U.S. President Donald Trump's administration talking up hopes for a deal that could reopen the crucial Strait of Hormuz.

"Even as news flow has been erratic, investors are gravitating towards the view that the worst may be over. Hopes are high that a US-Iran deal may be struck over the coming days," analysts at DBS said in a note.

DBS added India's recent FX curbs have raised hedging costs for foreign investors, which, along with disruption in onshore and NDF price discovery as well as volatility in FX swap implied yields, have weighed on foreign equity and debt flows.

On Thursday, Indian stocks ended a tad lower, lagging global peers, while the yield on the 10-year benchmark bond (IN064835G=CC) ticked up after the government included the paper in its first switch operation of the year.

Overseas investors have net sold over $6.5 billion of stocks and bonds in April so far.

Participants in a Reuters FX poll indicated that investors are most bearish on the rupee among Asian currencies, despite a marginal improvement in sentiment on prospects of de-escalation in the Middle East conflict.

data.title: Australia's Viva Energy Imports First Diesel Shipments Under Strategic Reserve — OPIS
data.description:
Viva Energy has procured two shipments totaling around 100 million liters of diesel from Brunei and South Korea by partnering with Export Finance Australia under the country's new Strategic Reserve bill, according to a news release by the Ministry for Climate Change and Energy on Thursday.

The bill, which was enacted on April 1, grants EFA the authority to underwrite purchases of fuel imports, thus allowing the government to aid Australian importers in their procurements at a time of surging fuel prices.

It was not stated how much funding was provided by EFA for these purchases and when the volumes will be delivered.

The vessel Tabit was loaded with 38,400 metric tons of diesel at Muara port, Brunei, on April 13 and is expected to reach West Australia on April 21, according to data provider Vortexa. However, it could not be determined if this cargo is one of the two cargoes imported by Viva Energy.

EFA will also partner other Australian oil firms, such as Ampol, Park Fuels and IOR, for their procurements.

"These cargoes are additional to existing contracted supply, are required to remain in Australia and will be directed to the industries and regions which need them the most," said Minister for Climate Change and Energy Chris Bowen.

This content was created by Oil Price Information Service, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. OPIS is run independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

  • Reporting by Kite Chong, kchong@opisnet.com; Editing by Mei-Hwen Wong, mwong@opisnet.com

data.title: abrdn National Municipal Income Fund to drop 20% cap, may raise high-yield muni exposure to 30–50%
data.description:
abrdn National Municipal Income Fund approved removal of its 20% cap on below-investment-grade municipal securities, effective June 1, 2026.

Key Highlights:

  • Board removed non-fundamental limit allowing up to 100% investment in below-investment-grade municipal obligations.
  • Change effective June 1, 2026; Fund may invest without a specific cap in high-yield municipal securities.
  • Fund expects to increase high-yield exposure initially to ~30%, and to ~30%–50% over time depending on markets.
  • Removal may raise credit exposure and volatility; high-yield munis are less liquid and more speculative.

Original SEC Filing: abrdn National Municipal Income Fund [ VFL ] - 8-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: Stellantis to Invest $118 Million in Repurposing French Vehicle Production Plant
data.description:
By Dominic Chopping

Stellantis will wind down vehicle production at its Poissy plant near Paris as it scales back European manufacturing capacity, but said the factory will remain open as it unveiled plans to repurpose the site.

The Jeep maker said Thursday it will invest 100 million euros ($118 million) to retool the site for auto parts production and recycling.

"The site will not close," Stellantis said in a statement. "It will have a sustainable industrial future."

The company currently assembles the Opel Mokka and DS 3 models at Poissy, with production continuing through at least the end of 2028. It will then switch to the new activities which are expected to be fully operational by 2030.

Around 1,600 workers currently work onsite at Poissy, which will drop to around 1,200 when the plant is repurposed. Headcount reductions will be managed through natural attrition or individual voluntary measures.

"In an automotive industry undergoing profound transformation, this project, co-designed with the social partners, ensures a sustainable future for the Poissy industrial site, built around activities clearly focused on the future," said Xavier Chereau, chief human resources and sustainability officer at Stellantis.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: PepsiCo reports Q1 2026: Revenue $19.44B, EPS $1.70, organic revenue +2.6%
data.description:
PepsiCo reported first-quarter 2026 results with net revenue of $19.443 billion, up 8.5% year-over-year, and diluted earnings per share of $1.70, a 27% increase versus the prior year. Organic revenue rose 2.6% and core EPS was $1.61 (core constant currency EPS +5%), while operating profit expanded to $3.213 billion with a 16.5% operating margin. The company affirmed its fiscal 2026 guidance, including organic revenue growth of 2–4% and core constant currency EPS growth of 4–6%.

Financial Highlights

  • Net revenue: $19,443 million for Q1 2026, up 8.5% from $17,919 million in Q1 2025.
  • Gross profit: $10,731 million for Q1 2026 (reported gross margin 55.2%).
  • Operating profit: $3,213 million for Q1 2026; operating margin 16.5% (up 210 bps).
  • Net income attributable to PepsiCo: $2,327 million for Q1 2026.
  • Diluted net income per common share: $1.70 for Q1 2026; Core EPS: $1.61 (core constant currency EPS +5%).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth drivers included a 3.4-percentage-point foreign exchange translation tailwind and a 2.5-percentage-point net benefit from acquisitions/divestitures, with organic revenue growth of 2.6% driven by effective net pricing and slight organic volume gains.
  • North America showed sequential acceleration in reported and organic revenue growth; PepsiCo Foods delivered volume growth supported by innovation and affordability initiatives, and PepsiCo Beverages improved sequential volume trends.
  • International operations performed resiliently with sequential net revenue acceleration across segments; Asia Pacific Foods, EMEA and International Beverages Franchise contributed notably to organic revenue growth.
  • Productivity savings and tightly managed costs supported core operating profit growth; management highlighted execution of a commercial agenda including brand restaging, innovation activity and affordability initiatives.
  • Cash generation and capital allocation: company affirmed fiscal 2026 targets including capital spending below 5% of net revenue, free cash flow conversion ratio of at least 80%, and total cash returns to shareholders of approximately $8.9 billion (dividends ~$7.9B; repurchases ~$1.0B).

Original SEC Filing: PEPSICO INC [ PEP ] - 8-K - Apr. 15, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Lincoln Educational Services Secures $125 Million Five-Year Revolving Credit Facility With Fifth Third and Lenders
data.description:
Lincoln Educational Services amended and restated its credit arrangements, upsizing its revolving credit facility to $125 million and extending its term to April 11, 2031. Fifth Third Bank serves as administrative agent alongside Flagstar Bank, Provident Bank and Santander Bank. The facility includes a $10 million letter of credit sublimit and a $25 million accordion, with interest based on SOFR or the Base Rate plus a leverage-based margin. The company also amended and restated its guaranty and security agreement, granting a first-priority lien on substantially all personal property to secure the facility, enhancing liquidity for working capital and general corporate purposes.

Agreement 1: Lincoln Educational Services Secures $125 Million Five-Year Revolving Credit Facility With Fifth Third and Lenders

  • Agreement type: Secured five-year revolving credit facility ($125 million; $10 million L/C sublimit; $25 million accordion)
  • Counterparty: Fifth Third Bank as administrative agent, and Flagstar Bank, Provident Bank, Santander Bank
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 13 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years (to Apr 11 2031)
  • Reason: Increase liquidity and flexibility for working capital and general purposes

Agreement 2: Lincoln Educational Services Amends Guaranty and Security Agreement Backing Credit Facility

  • Agreement type: Amended and restated guaranty and security agreement securing the facility
  • Counterparty: Fifth Third Bank as agent for the lenders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 13 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: 5 years (to Apr 11 2031)
  • Reason: Secure obligations under the new credit facility

Original SEC Filing: LINCOLN EDUCATIONAL SERVICES CORP [ LINC ] - 8-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: Equitable explores pre-merger common stock repurchases amid pending Corebridge transaction
data.description:
Equitable is exploring repurchasing common stock prior to closing of its pending merger with Corebridge, subject to a waiver from Corebridge.

Key Highlights:

  • Company may repurchase shares between filing of the preliminary proxy/prospectus and its mailing if it chooses to proceed.
  • Any repurchases would require a waiver from Corebridge of the merger agreement restriction on repurchases.
  • Volume, timing, pricing and method of any repurchases would be at Equitable’s discretion; no assurance repurchases will occur.
  • Filing includes forward-looking caution noting risks to timing, completion and benefits of the Proposed Transaction.

Original SEC Filing: Equitable Holdings, Inc. [ EQH ] - 8-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: American appoints Mark LaVerghetta to board as Thomas Sauve steps down
data.description:
American appointed Mark LaVerghetta to its board and nominating committee, effective April 15, 2026, as Thomas Sauve stepped down as a director the same day.

IndividualMark LaVerghettaThomas Sauve
RoleDirector; member of the Nominating CommitteeDirector (formerly President and officer; now non-officer employee in business strategy and development)
Type of ChangeAppointedStepped down/resigned from board
Effective DateApril 15, 2026April 15, 2026 (had previously tendered resignation as President/officer on December 25, 2025)

data.title: AMC Robotics delays 2025 Form 10-K filing due to warrant accounting
data.description:
AMC Robotics announced a delay in filing its Form 10-K for the year ended Dec. 31, 2025 to finalize accounting for warrants.

Key Highlights:

  • Company will miss the April 15, 2026 Form 10-K deadline due to additional accounting work on warrants.
  • Delay stems from determining appropriate U.S. GAAP classification and disclosure for warrants from prior private financing.
  • Company and advisors are working to finalize accounting; issue described as technical and not reflecting business performance.
  • Press release furnished under Regulation FD; exhibit 99.1 provides the announcement and investor contact details.

Original SEC Filing: AMC Robotics Corp [ AMCI ] - 8-K - Apr. 15, 2026

data.title: German car buyers keen on Chinese EV maker BYD in first quarter, data shows
data.description:
By Rachel More and Christina Amann

Chinese car brands such as BYD are gaining traction among German consumers who are increasingly looking to buy electric cars amid rising fuel prices, according to online marketplace data seen by Reuters on Thursday.

BYD was one of the fastest-growing brands in Germany in the first quarter of the year, online marketplace Carwow said, citing a 135% rise in purchase queries for the Chinese EV heavyweight during that period.

The data showed strong interest in BYD's electric-powered SUVs and the low-cost Dolphin hatchback, which have put pressure on European rivals to produce more affordable alternatives.

Carwow said Chinese brands look set to profit from higher prices at the pump due to the Middle East conflict and rising prices for new cars, with Chinese-owned carmaker MG also seeing a boost on its platform.

"Affordable electric cars with short delivery times are thus becoming significantly more attractive — an environment in which Chinese manufacturers, in particular, are capitalising on their strengths and noticeably gaining market share," the company said.

Queries for battery-electric vehicles in general rose by around 184% in the first three months of 2026 compared to the previous quarter, according to the data.

The increased interest is also translating into higher sales in Germany, where European brands dominate.

BYD saw registrations surge by 327% in March, according to the KBA regulator, making for a market share of 1.2% that month - still far behind Volkswagen's 17.9% share and other local carmakers.

Following low uptake of Chinese models over the past quarters, "Q1 2026 provided the first genuine signs that private uptake is starting to bite", independent automotive anlayst Matthias Schmidt told Reuters.

However, he pointed out that German carmakers are fighting back "with an accelerating product cadence, particularly in the second half of the year".
